stories.headline: Constellation enters 5 GW of nuclear, gas and battery projects in PJM queue
stories.summary: On its Q1 earnings call, Constellation said it placed nuclear uprates, gas-fired generation and battery storage totaling roughly 5 GW into PJM's interconnection queue while waiting for more detail on PJM's backstop colocation proposal.

stories.headline: PPL 'advanced' PA data center pipeline reaches 28.3 GW
stories.summary: PPL Electric's advanced-stage data center pipeline in Pennsylvania grew to 28.3 GW, up 12% in a quarter, with about 5 GW already under construction and contracts in place with QTS, AWS, PowerHouse and CoreWeave; Western Hub real-time LMP sat at $28.37/MWh this morning with PJM load forecast at 89.5 GW.

stories.headline: PJM, SPP, CAISO say they can meet summer demand
stories.summary: Three RTOs covering most of the eastern and western U.S. told stakeholders their summer assessments show enough generation to cover forecast peak load, easing reliability concerns ahead of cooling season.

stories.headline: Regulator: biggest U.S. grid may be too large to function
stories.summary: A sitting regulator told Bloomberg PJM's footprint — 13 states plus DC — has grown unwieldy enough to threaten effective oversight, an extraordinary public concession as PJM struggles with capacity-market price spikes and colocation litigation.

stories.headline: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed chair this afternoon
stories.summary: Warsh's 2:30 p.m. confirmation vote caps a fast-track Senate confirmation pace under Thune; Democrats on Banking warn his thin bipartisan ties on the Hill could complicate the Fed if the chamber flips.

stories.headline: House Republicans cool to Trump's federal gas tax holiday push
stories.summary: With WTI near $102/bbl on the Iran war, Thune is 'all but dismissing' Trump's call to pause the federal gas tax and Speaker Johnson called it merely 'intriguing,' the latest sign GOP leaders are ignoring White House asks on affordability.

stories.headline: Senate to vote on Iran war powers resolution at 11:30 a.m.
stories.summary: Sen. Jeff Merkley's (D-Ore.) war powers resolution gets a floor vote as Trump arrives in China for a Xi summit dominated by the still-closed Strait of Hormuz and Iran fighting that's driven gasoline prices to record highs.

stories.headline: House votes today on year-round E15 ethanol sales
stories.summary: The House is expected to take up — and Republicans expect to fail — legislation authorizing year-round nationwide sales of 15% ethanol fuel, a perennial Midwestern ag-state priority with downstream fuel-supply implications.

stories.headline: PA-03: Street, Rabb, Stanford face off in Tuesday's primary
stories.summary: With six days until the May 19 primary in Pennsylvania's most Democratic-leaning seat, the Inquirer contrasts the legislative styles of state Sen. Sharif Street and Rep. Chris Rabb, while physician Ala Stanford carries Rep. Dwight Evans's endorsement.

stories.headline: PA Supreme Court Justice Wecht ditches Democratic Party
stories.summary: Justice David Wecht said he is 'no longer registered within any political party,' a move Sen. John Fetterman publicly defended; Franklin & Marshall's Berwood Yost called the timing optimal because Wecht is past retention politics.

stories.headline: PA man arrested for 'politically violent' threats against 20 state lawmakers
stories.summary: State Police charged 42-year-old Adam G. Berryhill of Lebanon County with terroristic threats after he allegedly posted a 'hit list' and referenced a 'Memorial Day Operation' targeting 20 Democratic House and Senate members on X in late April.

stories.headline: 145,000 Pennsylvanians have dropped ACA coverage amid premium spikes
stories.summary: More than 145,000 PA residents have canceled Affordable Care Act coverage in recent months after monthly premiums jumped, a steady enrollment decline state officials expect to continue through 2026 — fallout from the lapsed enhanced subsidies.

stories.headline: Pirates 3, Rockies 1: Skenes nearly no-hits Colorado
stories.summary: Paul Skenes carried a no-hitter into the seventh before Mickey Moniak singled, finishing 8 IP, 2 H, 0 R, 10 K; Oneil Cruz doubled twice and scored both Pirates runs on RBI singles from Nick Gonzales and Brandon Lowe to lift Pittsburgh to 23-19.

stories.headline: Dubas: Penguins 'would love' to re-sign Malkin; Crosby extension eligible July 1
stories.summary: GM Kyle Dubas's season-ending presser at UPMC Lemieux signaled continued talks with Malkin's agent J.P. Barry on at least one more year, said Crosby becomes extension-eligible July 1, and conceded the Penguins are 'well short' of contender status with defense the focus.
